mappings.reason: Uninstalling or disabling unnecessary services on servers directly enforces the single-function-per-server requirement by removing extraneous service functions from enterprise assets.
Anti-exploitation features like DEP and WDEG enforce separate execution domains for processes at the OS level, directly implementing the process isolation mechanism.
Dedicated computing resources separated physically or logically for administrative tasks implements the single-function principle by segregating privileged work from standard operations.
Secure network architecture addressing segmentation supports server function separation by designing isolated network segments that can enforce single-function server deployment.
